info.title: Number Acquisition
info.description:
Bandwidth's Number Acquisition API

This API currently provides endpoints to order phone numbers via a New Number Order.

get.description:
Retrieve order IDs and order details for previously attempted new phone number orders. A maximum of 1,000 orders can be retrieved per request. If no date range or specific query parameter (marked by * below) is provided, the order results will be limited to the last two years.

This request will return a list of the orders that match the criteria imposed by the provided query parameters. The orders list contains a detailed description of each order. Notice that the first elements of the payload provide link information that can be used to page through a paginated list of orders. Pagination can be controlled by use of the size and page query parameters.

This order returns a 204: No Content when the endpoint finds no results matching the parameters provided.

post.description:
A POST to the /orders resource is used to request that the system provide one or more phone number(s) for use by the account. The post creates a new number order record to preserve the request, as well as the response of the Bandwidth App API to the request. A well-formed POST on the /orders resource will create an order record, and return an order ID string that can be used to uniquely identify the new number order request. Capabilities that are not universally available are defined in the table below. The following request schemas and parameters are only allowed for numbers from specific region/country: | Number Region | Request Object Type | Request Parameter | |:--------------|:------------------|------------------| |NANP|rateCenterSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|areaCodeSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|npaNxxSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|tollFreeWildCharSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|tollFreeVanitySearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|lataSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|zipSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|citySearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|stateSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|combinedSearchAndOrderType|-| |NANP|-|backOrderRequested| |NANP|-|closeOrder| |NANP|-|protected TnAttribute| |NANP|-|reservationIdList in ExistingTelephoneNumberOrderType schema|

Please visit Guides and Tutorials to learn more.

get.description: Retrieves a total number of phone number orders.
get.summary: Get New Number Order Phone Number Count Totals

get.description:
Get all of the details associated with an identified order.

Please visit Guides and Tutorials to learn more.

put.description:
For orders that support Backorder capabilities, new number orders may stay in backordered state while the order is filled. While in this state it is possible to update the modifiable fields in the record, as well as to request that backorder processing of the order be ended. The fields that can be updated are...
  • The order name
  • The customer order ID
  • The backordered state.
Specifying a <CloseOrder> element (XML) or "closeOrder" key (JSON) with a value of true will cancel the backorder request, leaving the currently ordered numbers on the account. No further numbers will be added to the account as a result of the order.

get.description: Retrieves the area codes of the phone numbers from the specified order.
get.summary: List New Number Order Area Codes [NANP]

get.description: Retrieve a full history of updates and changes associated with an order.

Retrieve all notes associated with the order.

This order returns a 204: No Content when the endpoint finds no results matching the parameters provided.

post.description: Add a note to an order. This causes a notification to be sent to Bandwidth Operations, so that they may assist as necessary. A note may be up to 500 characters in length.

put.description: Update a specified note. Notes may only be updated, not deleted.

get.description: Retrieves the Npa-Nxx of the phone numbers from the specified order.
Returns a list of phone numbers associated with the ID of a given new number order.
get.summary: List Phone Numbers from a New Number Order

get.description: Retrieves the total quantity of phone numbers from the specified new number order.
get.summary: Get Quantity Of Phone Numbers In New Number Order

get.description:
The /cities resource returns all the cities in the indicated state that are supported from a coverage perspective and, if requested, that contain orderable inventory. The values returned indicate the names of the cities and, if available is specified, then returns the available inventory by city. Note that the mapping between rate center and city is not completely predictable because multiple cities can be served by a single rate center and it may take multiple rate centers to serve a city. The rate centers results are more predictable because they are defined strictly in telecommunication terms. The rate centers and cities API calls return information about the Bandwidth CLEC network, including coverage data, indicating both the extent of on-net and off-net coverage, as well as number availability, again from an on-net and off-net perspective. The rules are as follows:
  • If supported=true is specified, then the coverage or availability is reported for the Bandwidth CLEC only.
  • If supported is omitted or false, then the coverage or availability is reported for the combination of the Bandwidth CLEC and our partners.
  • If available=true is specified, then only rate centers in which we have available numbers are reported.
  • If available is omitted or false, then all rate centers within the scope defined by supported will be returned.
These rules apply to both the /rateCenters and /cities API calls.
Supported = true Supported is missing
available = true Available numbers within the Bandwidth CLEC network Available numbers within the Bandwidth CLEC network combined with our partner networks
available is missing Coverage in the Bandwidth CLEC Coverage of the combined Bandwidth CLEC + Partner networks

The rateCenters resource returns all the rate centers in the indicated state that are supported from a coverage perspective and, if requested, that contain orderable inventory. The values returned indicate the names of the rate centers and, if the query parameter available is specified, then returns the available inventory by rate center. The rate centers API call returns information about the Bandwidth CLEC network, including coverage data, indicating both the extent of on-net and off-net coverage, as well as number availability, again from an on-net and off-net perspective. The rules are as follows:
  • If supported=true is specified, then the coverage or availability is reported for the Bandwidth CLEC only.
  • If supported=true is omitted or false, then the coverage or availability is reported for the combination of the Bandwidth CLEC and our partners.
  • If available=true is specified, then only rate centers in which we have available numbers are reported.
  • If available is omitted or false, then all rate centers within the scope defined by supported will be returned.
These rules apply to both the /rateCenters and /cities API calls.
Supported = true Supported is missing
available = true Available numbers within the Bandwidth CLEC network Available numbers within the Bandwidth CLEC network combined with our partner networks
available is missing Coverage in the Bandwidth CLEC Coverage of the combined Bandwidth CLEC + Partner networks
The Covered Rate Centers API returns information about the Bandwidth CLEC network, including coverage data. This indicates the extent of on-net and off-net coverage, as well as number availability.
Get information about a covered rate center by ID.
Retrieves a list of available Npa-Nxx phone numbers.

requiredPageQueryParam.description: An ID of the first element in a page.  This value will indicate the first value, not the page number, of the initial entry in the page being requested. The initial page is tagged with the ID `1` as a convention.
requiredPageQueryParam.name: page

startDateQueryParam.description: The starting date of a search in yy-mm-dd format. startdate and enddate must be used as a pair.
startDateQueryParam.name: startDate

requiredSizeQueryParam.description: The number of elements to retrieve
requiredSizeQueryParam.name: size

endDateQueryParam.description: The ending date of a search in yy-mm-dd format. startdate and enddate must be used as a pair.
endDateQueryParam.name: endDate

customerOrderIdQueryParam.description: The Customer Order ID is an ID assigned by the account owner to provide a reference number for the order.
customerOrderIdQueryParam.name: customerOrderId

newPhoneNumberOrderStatusQueryParam.description: The status of the orders that should be in the returned set
newPhoneNumberOrderStatusQueryParam.name: status

userIdQueryParam.description: The userId that submitted the order
userIdQueryParam.name: userId

newPhoneNumberOrderPhoneNumberDetailQuer.description: if true, an extended payload describing details about the ordered phone numbers will be provided
newPhoneNumberOrderPhoneNumberDetailQuer.name: tndetail

statusQueryParam.description: The status of an order. Possible values are RECEIVED, COMPLETE, PARTIAL, BACKORDERED, FAILED, and PROCESSING.
statusQueryParam.name: status

availableCityQueryParam.name: available
availableCityQueryParam.description: If set to true, the payload includes the quantity of the available phone numbers in that city.

supportedQueryParam.name: supported
supportedQueryParam.description: If set to true, the payload will show rate centers that Bandwidth serves directly.

availableRateCenterQueryParam.name: available
availableRateCenterQueryParam.description: If set to true, the payload includes the quantity of the available phone numbers in that rate center.

pageQueryParam.name: page
pageQueryParam.description: The starting value for a paginated response. The default is '1', indicating the first page of results.

sizeQueryParam.name: size
sizeQueryParam.description: The number of rate centers to include in a paginated result payload.

embedQueryParam.name: embed
embedQueryParam.description: A set of fields to be included in the response. Lists of zip codes, cities, vendors, NpaNxxXs, and counts of available or limited available phone numbers are not included for each rate center by default. For example, if the payload should contain a list of the cities covered by the rate centers, then embed=cities would be included as a query parameter. A list of vendors is available only for admin users. No filter parameters are supported if LocalRateCenters is specified. In this case, only size, page, and any other embed parameters are supported.

nameQueryParam.name: name
nameQueryParam.description: The name of a rate center.

zipQueryParam.name: zip
zipQueryParam.description: A 5 digit ZIP code of the area covered by the rate center.

cityQueryParam.name: city
cityQueryParam.description: The name of a city covered by the rate center.

lataQueryParam.name: lata
lataQueryParam.description: A 3 or 5 digit rate center's LATA.

tierQueryParam.name: tier
tierQueryParam.description: The tier associated with covered rate center; a value from 0 to 5.

npaQueryParam.name: npa
npaQueryParam.description: The 3 digit NPA or area code covered by the rate center.

npaNxxQueryParam.name: npaNxx
npaNxxQueryParam.description: The 6 digits NPA and NXX values covered by the rate center.

npaNxxXQueryParam.name: npaNxxX
npaNxxXQueryParam.description: The 7 digits of the NPA, NXX, and thousands-block values covered by the rate center.

areaCodeQueryParam.name: areaCode
areaCodeQueryParam.description: The allowed number ranges are [2-9] for the first digit and [0-9] for both the second and third digits.

quantityQueryParam.name: quantity
quantityQueryParam.description: Filters out any Npa-Nxx grouped phone numbers whose quantity falls short of the given quantity.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderCombinedSearchA.description:
Specify areaCode and localVanity to order phone numbers matching a specific alphanumeric pattern between 4 and 7 characters long.
Limitations: areaCode is required when searching for a local vanity number. No other parameter combinations are allowed here. This order type is unsupported with backOrderRequested=true.
combinedSearchAndOrderType on a number in Protected status

Specify any combination of: areaCode, rateCenter, state, npaNxx, npaNxxX, lata, city, and zip, with the following limitations: areaCode, npaNxx and npaNxxX are mutually exclusive; LCA search is supported only for one of the following criteria: npaNxx, npaNxxX, rateCenter and state; if city or rateCenter is specified, then state is required.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderExistingPhoneNu.description: Order a set of phone numbers known to be available. Typically, when the numbers have been found and/or reserved using a separate availableNumbers search. If the numbers have previously been reserved, a reservation ID must be included for each number.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderAreaCodeSearchA.description: Allowed ranges ~ [2-9] for the first digit and [0, 9] for both the second and third digits.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderCitySearchAndOr.description: Specify the City and State to be ordered from.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderCombinedSearchA.description:
Specify AreaCode and LocalVanity to order phone numbers matching a specific alphanumeric pattern between 4 and 7 characters long.
Limitations: AreaCode is always required. No parameter combinations are allowed here/ This order type is unsupported with BackOrderRequested=true.

Specify the any combination of: AreaCode, RateCenter, State, NpaNxx, NpaNxxX, Lata, City, Zip. But with following limitations: AreaCode, NpaNxx and NpaNxxX are mutually exclusive, LCA search is supported only for one of the following criteria: NpaNxx, NpaNxxX, RateCenter and State. If City or RateCenter is specified, then State is required.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderExistingTelepho.description: Order a set of phone numbers known to be available. This often results if the numbers have been found using a separate availableNumbers search. If the numbers have previously been reserved, a reservation ID must be included

createNewPhoneNumberOrderGeneralXmlExamp.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<Order>
    <CustomerOrderId>SJM00001</CustomerOrderId>
    <!--
        There are various types of orders, all of which are submitted in the 'xxxOrderType'
        element of the Order Request payload. Replace
        this comment to create a complete payload that includes the order type and the
        common elements.
        Take a look at the example payloads as well.
    -->
    <SiteId>202</SiteId>
    <PeerId>518824</PeerId>
    <VoiceConfigurationPackageId>d21315af-9b5b-4b45-a6f4-9106cbfe87d1</VoiceConfigurationPackageId>
    <RequirementsPackageId>3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6</RequirementsPackageId>
    <VoiceIntegrationIds>
      <VoiceIntegrationId>5fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6</VoiceIntegrationId>
      <VoiceIntegrationId>5fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa7</VoiceIntegrationId>
    </VoiceIntegrationIds>
    <AutoActivate>true</AutoActivate>
    <TnAttributes/>
    <Tags>
      <Tag>
        <TagName>customerId</TagName>
        <TagValue>11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111</TagValue>
      </Tag>
    </Tags>
</Order>

createNewPhoneNumberOrderLataSearchAndOr.description: Specify the LATA to order phone numbers from.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderNpaNxxSearchAnd.description:
Specify the NpaNxx combination to be ordered. Valid Npa values ~ [2-9] for the first digit, and [0-9] for both the second and third digits. Valid Nxx values ~ [2-9] for the first digit, and [0-9] for both the second and third digits. A similar approach is viable for NPANXXXX. The EnableLCA flag turns LCA search on or off.

Limitations:
These order types are unsupported with BackOrderRequested=true

createNewPhoneNumberOrderRateCenterSearc.description: Specify the Rate Center and the State.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderStateSearchAndO.description: Specify the State to be searched for phone numbers.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderTollFreeVanityS.description: Specify a Toll Free Vanity search, where the numbers ordered match a specific alphanumeric pattern between 4 and 7 characters long.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderTollFreeWildCha.description: Specify the Toll Free wild card pattern. To be ordered, comprised of 3 digits beginning with '8'. Examples are 8**, 87*, etc.

createNewPhoneNumberOrderZipSearchAndOrd.description: Specify the Zip Code to be ordered from.

updateNewPhoneNumberOrderJsonExample.description: Request to update an existing New Number Order using Json

updateNewPhoneNumberOrderXmlExample.description: Request to update an existing New Number Order using XML
updateNewPhoneNumberOrderXmlExample.value:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<Order>
    <Name>Available Phone Number order</Name>    
    <CustomerOrderId>123456789</CustomerOrderId>
<!-- Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 40 characters. -->
    <CloseOrder>true</CloseOrder>
</Order>

<Note>

    <!-- note the userId must match the logged-in user -->
    <Description>note text</Description>
</Note>

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between this link and the resource.

Description.description: This is the Order Note description.

Id.description: This is the Order Note identifier that is automatically generated by system.

LastDateModifier.description: This is the date when Order Note was last modified.

UserId.description: The UserId is authenticated for access to the network via a username string. It is required for UserId to match the logged-in user when making a new note.

first.description: The first (or only) page of results matching the query.

last.description: If more results exist than specified by 'size', this link return the last page of result.

next.description: If more results exist than specified by 'size', this link returns the next page of 'size' results.

previous.description: If the results are more than one page, this link returns the previous page of 'size' results.

AutoActivate.description: AutoActivate is used to indicate whether the service should be activated upon completion of the order. If not specified, the default value is true.
AutoActivate is used to indicate whether the service should be activated upon completion of the order. If not specified, the default value is true.

tnAttribute.description: The TnAttributes field specifies line attributes that will apply to the ported in phone numbers. If present, TnAttributes may have a value of PROTECTED. This element is not applicable for off-net and toll free port-types.

voiceConfigurationPackageId.description: `voiceConfigurationPackageId` is the identifier of the voice configuration package containing the voice origination settings. Upon activation, phone numbers will be configured with the voice origination settings specified in the voice configuration package.
voiceConfigurationPackageId.title: Voice Configuration Package ID

voiceIntegrationIdsJson.description:
A list of voice integration identifiers to associate with the phone numbers upon activation, 
which will configure them with the specified voice integration settings.
This creates a number syncing request that can be tracked with the bulkNumberSyncId.
voiceIntegrationIdsJson.title: Voice Integrations

voiceIntegrationIds.description:
A list of voice integration identifiers to associate with the phone numbers upon activation, 
which will configure them with the specified voice integration settings.
This creates a number syncing request that can be tracked with the bulkNumberSyncId.

bulkNumberSyncId.description: Adding voice integrations to the phone number(s) being ordered automatically kicks off an asynchronous bulk provisioning task. Use this ID to check the status of the provisioning request [here](https://dev.bandwidth.com/apis/universal-platform/voice-provisioning/#tag/Bulk-Voice-Phone-Number-Provisioning/operation/getBulkVoicePhoneNumbers).
bulkNumberSyncId.title: Bulk Number Sync ID

tagName.description: Name of a tag to associate with a phone number. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and underscores are allowed. Between 1 and 255 characters. Is case sensitive.

tagValue.description: Value of a tag to associate with a phone number. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and underscores are allowed. Between 1 and 255 characters. Is case sensitive.

phoneNumberTag.description: A tag to associate with the phone numbers being ordered.

phoneNumberTagXml.description: A tag to associate with the phone numbers being ordered.

phoneNumberTags.description: Tags to associate with the phone numbers being ordered on order completion. For `PARTIAL` or `FAILED` orders, the tags will not be applied to phone numbers which are not successfully added to your account.

phoneNumberTagsXml.description: Tags to associate with the phone numbers being ordered on order completion. For `PARTIAL` or `FAILED` orders, the tags will not be applied to phone numbers which are not successfully added to your account.

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`.
Example: `Authorization: Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security 
tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer
of this token.” The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to
protected resources.

- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.

For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/credentials)

tags.name: New Number Orders
tags.description:
The Number Order API ("Orders") lets users procure phone numbers and add them to their Bandwidth account. Number Orders are asynchronous: when users submit a POST request, they receive an OrderId that they can poll or subscribe to updates for until the order reaches a final state. Users can procure numbers in several ways with the Number Order API:

  • Search and Order: Provide search criteria and purchase numbers that match.
  • Existing Number Order: Provide a list of specific numbers, and if they're still available, they'll be added to the account.
  • Backordering: If numbers matching the specified criteria aren't currently available, they can be placed in backorder and Bandwidth will fulfill it automatically once they become available. Users can also use the Order Notification Webhook to receive callbacks or email notifications as the order progresses.
Related resources:
Guide to Searching and Ordering Numbers
Order Notification Webhook

tags.name: Coverage
tags.description: These APIs provide information related to Bandwidth's geographic coverage and phone number types supported, as well as the availability of phone number inventory.
